function_explanation: Computes travel-time residuals using a straight-ray approximation by multiplying a sparse ray-path kernel matrix with the slowness perturbation derived from velocity perturbation and reference velocity.
function_docstring:
Compute travel-time residuals using straight-ray approximation.

This function implements the forward model δt = G @ (δm / c0), where G is
the ray-path kernel matrix and δm is the slowness perturbation. It computes
the predicted travel-time residuals for seismic rays passing through a
velocity model.

Args:
    G: Sparse kernel matrix with shape (n_rays, n_cells) representing the
        ray-path sensitivities. Each row corresponds to a ray and each
        column to a model cell.
    dm_flat: Flattened velocity perturbation model with shape (n_cells,).
        Represents the deviation from the reference velocity model.
    c0: Reference phase velocity in km/s. Used to convert velocity
        perturbations to slowness perturbations.

Returns:
    dt: Travel-time residuals with shape (n_rays,). Each element represents
        the predicted travel-time perturbation for the corresponding ray.
usage_example:
import numpy as np
from scipy import sparse

# Create a simple ray-path kernel matrix (5 rays, 10 cells)
n_rays, n_cells = 5, 10
G = sparse.random(n_rays, n_cells, density=0.3, format='csr')

# Define velocity perturbation model
dm_flat = np.random.randn(n_cells) * 0.1  # Small perturbations

# Reference phase velocity in km/s
c0 = 3.5

# Compute travel-time residuals
dt = forward_operator(G, dm_flat, c0)
print(f"Travel-time residuals shape: {dt.shape}")  # (5,)
